revision.text:
[[ja:船小屋温泉]] {{infobox Onsen |Name = Funagoya Onsen |image =Funagoya Mineral spring place.jpg |Comment = Funagoya Mineral Spring Place |Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Chikugo, Fukuoka|en|Chikugo, Fukuoka|qid=Q601137|lt=Chikugo City|1=Chikugo, Fukuoka}} {{Switcher |{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300}} |Wikimedia maps を表示 |{{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=300}} |福岡県地図を表示 }} |Coordinates = {{coord|33|10|59.4|N|130|30|22.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} |Access = 鉄道 – {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=JR|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikugo-Funagoya Station|en|Chikugo-Funagoya Station|qid=Q1136543|lt=Chikugo Funagoya Station|1=Chikugo-Funagoya Station}}
バス – {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11629843}} 「船小屋」バス停
車 – {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|qid=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway|1=Kyushu Expressway}} {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|lt=Yame IC|lt_ja=八女IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1654393}} |Spring Quality = Iron‐containing carbonated spring |Spring Temperature = 19 |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Character = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Accommodation Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://www.funagoya.org/ 船小屋温泉郷公式サイト]
[http://www.city.chikugo.fukuoka.jp/ 筑後市公式サイト]
[http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/ みやま市公式サイト] |Notes = }} [[File:Nagata kousenjyo.jpg|right|thumb|'''{{ill|Nagata Onsen|ja|長田鉱泉場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Onsen|1=Nagata Kōsenjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11653497}}''']] '''Funagoya Onsen''' (ふなごやおんせん) is a cold spring ({{ill|Mineral spring|ja|冷鉱泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mineral spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鉱泉}}) that emerges along the Yabe River ({{ill|Yabe River|ja|矢部川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabe River|1=Yabe River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11584292}}) on the southern edge of {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} and {{ill|Chikugo, Fukuoka|en|Chikugo, Fukuoka|qid=Q601137|lt=Chikugo City|1=Chikugo, Fukuoka}}. It is renowned as one of Japan’s leading carbonated springs, notable for its high iron ({{ill|Iron|en|Iron|qid=Q677|lt=Iron|1=Iron}}) content.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://fukuoka-onsen.com/english/file/funagoya-wakita.pdf|title=船小屋温泉 脇田温泉|website=福岡県観光温泉地協会|accessdate=2024-10-22}} Although the spring emerges on the Chikugo City (right bank) side of the Yabe River, it also emerges on the opposite (left bank) side in {{ill|Miyama, Fukuoka|en|Miyama, Fukuoka|qid=Q990465|lt=Miyama City|1=Miyama, Fukuoka}}—where it is called '''Shin Funagoya Onsen'''. A bridge, the {{ill|Funagoya Onsen Bridge|ja|船小屋温泉大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagoya Onsen Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, spans the Yabe River, which is traversed by {{ill|Japan National Route 209|en|Japan National Route 209|qid=Q6156947|lt=National Route 209|1=Japan National Route 209}}. In this article both Funagoya Onsen and Shin Funagoya Onsen are described. == Spring Quality == Funagoya Onsen is an iron‐containing carbonated spring. Drinking water from an iron‐rich spring ({{ill|Drinking Spring|ja|飲泉|1=Hot Spring Drinking|de|Brunnenkur|lt=drinking hot spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Drinking Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Drinking|qq=}}) is generally considered beneficial for conditions such as {{ill|Iron-deficiency anemia|en|Iron-deficiency anemia|qid=Q954674|lt=Iron Deficiency Anemia|1=Iron-deficiency anemia}}, as it can help supplement iron levels.山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.13 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5 Because the mineral water contains iron, oxidation by atmospheric oxygen after emergence produces a reddish precipitate.日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.150 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8 It is noteworthy that in Fukuoka Prefecture most hot springs have relatively high source temperatures—with over 80% of springs being above 25 ℃ as of 2010.山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.231 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5 However, the source temperature at Funagoya Onsen is only 19 ℃; because it is below 25 ℃, it is classified as a cold spring.山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.3 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5 Due to its relatively low temperature, the dissolved carbon dioxide ({{ill|Carbonic acid|en|Carbonic acid|qid=Q104334|lt=Carbonic Acid|1=Carbonic acid}}) is not completely lost after emergence and remains partly dissolved in the water if observed immediately. === Origin of the Dissolved Carbon Dioxide and Water === Analyses using the ratios of [[炭素の同位体]] ({{ill|Isotopes of carbon|en|Isotopes of carbon|qid=Q471790|lt=Carbon Isotopes|1=Isotopes of carbon}}) in the cold spring water have revealed that approximately 56% of the dissolved carbonate originates from carbonate deposits subducted into the Earth by {{ill|Plate tectonics|en|Plate tectonics|qid=Q7950|lt=Plate Tectonics|1=Plate tectonics}}, about 35% from carbon dioxide newly upwelling from the Earth’s mantle, and roughly 9% from carbon dioxide produced by the decomposition of accumulated organic matter. Furthermore, combined analyses of the ratios of [[水素の同位体]] ({{ill|Isotopes of hydrogen|en|Isotopes of hydrogen|qid=Q466603|lt=Hydrogen Isotopes|1=Isotopes of hydrogen}}) and [[酸素の同位体]] ({{ill|Isotopes of oxygen|en|Isotopes of oxygen|qid=Q785643|lt=Oxygen Isotopes|1=Isotopes of oxygen}}) have shown that the water’s source is not fossil water derived from subducted seawater nor virgin water from the mantle, but rather groundwater recharged by rainfall. Thus, it is believed that rainwater percolates underground, is supplied with carbon dioxide from subducted carbonate deposits via the mantle and plate tectonics, and then emerges at the surface. In addition, analyses of trace [[ヘリウムの同位体]] ({{ill|Isotopes of helium|en|Isotopes of helium|qid=Q466960|lt=Helium Isotopes|1=Isotopes of helium}}) in the mineral water indicate that helium derived from the atmosphere is below 1 {{ill|Per mille|en|Per mille|qid=Q181011|lt=per mille|1=Per mille}}, whereas about 60% originates from the mantle and 40% from the crust. This also suggests that the mineral water at Funagoya Onsen incorporates gas components supplied from deep within the Earth. === Regarding the Contained Methane === In addition, the mineral water at Funagoya Onsen contains trace amounts of {{ill|Methane|en|Methane|qid=Q37129|lt=Methane|1=Methane}}. Although about 9% of the carbon dioxide in the water comes from decomposed organic matter, it is thought that a portion of this organic matter is converted into methane by certain microorganisms. == Hot Spring Area == Funagoya Onsen is a cold spring that emerges in the middle reaches of the Yabe River. A hot spring town has developed around the area, with roughly 10 inns and hotels along the Yabe River. Although the source is a cold spring, heated bath facilities are also available. On the Chikugo City side, the {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Chikugo Wide Area Park|ja|筑後広域公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Chikugo Wide Area Park|1=Chikugo Regional Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11602817}} has been established; within the park is a communal bathhouse called “Koi Botaru” that offers day-use bathing. The park extends further west than the {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|qid=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}, and it is possible to reach “Koi Botaru” from {{ill|Chikugo-Funagoya Station|en|Chikugo-Funagoya Station|qid=Q1136543|lt=Chikugo Funagoya Station|1=Chikugo-Funagoya Station}} via the park. In addition, Funagoya Onsen features a facility for drinking its mineral water, serving as a central amenity for the area. Facilities for drinking the mineral water are provided on both sides of the Yabe River—in Chikugo City, where drinking is free, and on the Miyama City side, where the {{ill|Nagata Onsen|ja|長田鉱泉場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Onsen|1=Nagata Kōsenjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11653497}} charges a fee. === Surroundings === Funagoya Onsen is located near the administrative boundary of Chikugo City and Miyama City along {{ill|Japan National Route 209|en|Japan National Route 209|qid=Q6156947|lt=National Route 209|1=Japan National Route 209}}, which crosses the Yabe River via the {{ill|Funagoya Onsen Bridge|ja|船小屋温泉大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagoya Onsen Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additionally, on the Miyama City side, {{ill|Nakanoshima Park|ja|中の島公園 (福岡県みやま市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanoshima Park|1=Q11362773|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11362773}} has been established. === Events in the Hot Spring Town === A fireworks festival is held annually by the Yabe River. Formerly called the “Funagoya Onsen Fireworks Festival,” in 2019 it was merged with the “Chiggo Festival” and renamed the “Chiggo Festival ~ Fireworks Festival of the Land of Love.” The festival was cancelled in 2020 and 2021 due to the spread of {{ill|COVID-19|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染拡大|lt=COVID-19|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新型コロナウイルス感染症の世界的流行 (2019年-)|1=COVID-19 pandemic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q81068910}}, held in 2022 under the name “Chiggo Koi no Kuni Fireworks Festival,” and cancelled again in 2023. === Access === * By Public Transportation: ** From {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=JR Kyushu|1=Kyushu Railway Company}}’s {{ill|Kyushu Shinkansen|en|Kyushu Shinkansen|qid=Q11370254|lt=Kyushu Shinkansen|1=Kyushu Shinkansen}} / {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|qid=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}} at {{ill|Chikugo-Funagoya Station|en|Chikugo-Funagoya Station|qid=Q1136543|lt=Chikugo Funagoya Station|1=Chikugo-Funagoya Station}}, it is about 1.2 km to the northeast. If using {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11629843}}, alight at the “Funagoya” bus stop. ** From {{ill|Kurume Station|en|Kurume Station|qid=Q1122628|lt=Kurume Station|1=Kurume Station}}, {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|en|Hainuzuka Station|qid=Q5359747|lt=Haneunizuka Station|1=Hainuzuka Station}}, or {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|en|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|qid=Q7040736|lt=Nishitetsu Kurume Station|1=Nishitetsu Kurume Station}}, take Nishitetsu Bus routes 50 or 53 bound for Funagoya, alight at the final stop, and then walk about 200 m toward the Yabe River. ** From {{ill|Setaka Station|en|Setaka Station|qid=Q5359332|lt=Setaka Station|1=Setaka Station}}, take the {{ill|Miyama City Community Bus|ja|みやま市コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama City Community Bus|1=Miyama City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109370490}} “Kusuppī-go” (Route 1) and alight at “Shin Funagoya.” (Note: This is the only public transport serving the Shin Funagoya area, but services are few and may be suspended on holidays.) * By Car: ** From {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|qid=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway|1=Kyushu Expressway}} at {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|lt=Yame IC|lt_ja=八女IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1654393}}, it is about 5 km southwest along {{ill|Japan National Route 209|en|Japan National Route 209|qid=Q6156947|lt=National Route 209|1=Japan National Route 209}}. == History == The hot spring was first opened in 1824 (Bunsei 7).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20241022/ddl/k40/040/242000c|title=船小屋鉱泉場リニューアル あずまや新築 地域の活性化期待　筑後／福岡|website=毎日新聞|accessdate=2024-10-22}} On October 20, 2024 (Reiwa 6), the facilities at the Funagoya Mineral Spring were renewed. == Footnotes == === Annotations === === Sources === == External Links == * [http://www.funagoya.org/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Funagoya Onsen Official Site|ja|船小屋温泉郷公式サイト}]] * [http://www.city.chikugo.fukuoka.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Chikugo City Official Site|ja|筑後市公式サイト}]] * [http://www.city.miyama.lg.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Miyama City Official Site|ja|みやま市公式サイト}]] {{ウィキポータルリンク|温泉}} [[File:Funagoya Mineral spring place.jpg|300px]] {{translated page|ja|船小屋温泉|version=102748591|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:船小屋温泉]] {{wikidata link|Q11614026}}
